cit:deliveryPoint.gco:CharacterString: Cnr Jerrabomberra Ave and Hindmarsh Dr GPO Box 378

cit:title.gco:CharacterString: Geoscience Australia Community Metadata Profile of ISO 19115-1:2014
Geoscience Australia Products Catalogue (eCat) – Short identifier for metadata record.
Summary of results. Joint NTGS–GA geochronology project: Aileron Province, Jinka and Dneiper 1:100 000 mapsheets, 2020

mri:abstract.gco:CharacterString: This Record presents new Sensitive High Resolution Ion Micro Probe (SHRIMP) U–Pb geochronological results from the Aileron Province that were obtained during the Northern Territory Geological Survey–Geoscience Australia (NTGS–GA) geochronology project under the National Collaboration Framework (NCF) agreement, in July 2020. Geoscience Australia’s contribution to this project forms part of the Exploring for the Future (EFTF) Program, which aims to better understand the mineral, energy, and groundwater resources of Northern Australia. Bibliographic Reference: Kositcin N, Beyer EE and Reno BL, 2021. Summary of results. Joint NTGS–GA geochronology project: Aileron Province, Jinka and Dneiper 1:100 000 mapsheets, 2020. Northern Territory Geological Survey, Record 2021-008.

mri:purpose.gco:CharacterString: To publicly report geochronology results produced by GA in collaboration with the Northern Territory Geological Survey

cit:deliveryPoint.gco:CharacterString: Cnr Jerrabomberra Ave and Hindmarsh Dr GPO Box 378

cit:name.gco:CharacterString: Data Store directory containing the digital product files

cit:description.gco:CharacterString: Data Store directory containing one or more files, possibly in a variety of formats, accessible to Geoscience Australia staff only for internal purposes

mco:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: © Northern Territory of Australia (Northern Territory Geological Survey) and Commonwealth of Australia (Geoscience Australia) 2021

cit:deliveryPoint.gco:CharacterString: Cnr Jerrabomberra Ave and Hindmarsh Dr

mrl:statement.gco:CharacterString: This work is part of an ongoing Geochronology Project, conducted by the Northern Territory Geological Survey (NTGS) and Geoscience Australia (GA) under a National Collaborative Framework agreement.
